note: Took office Jan 2025, defeating incumbent John Wilcher.
Sheriff since 1999; ran and re-elected as an Independent in 2024.
Sworn in Dec 27, 2024, as Dougherty County's first Black sheriff.
Took office Jan 2025, succeeding the late longtime Sheriff Cullen Talton.
Took office Jan 1, 2025, defeating longtime Sheriff Richard Roundtree (who died April 2026).
Sworn in Dec 2024; first Republican (and first Black) sheriff of Thomas County.
Elected sheriff (R, re-elected 2024); suspended by Gov. Kemp Oct 2025 and again June 29, 2026; Anthony Coleman serving as interim/acting sheriff as of 2026-07-03.

159 sheriff records returned, covering all 159 Georgia counties — no counties omitted. All names sourced from the Georgia Sheriffs' Association 2026 Annual Directory (published Jan 2026); ~48 counties independently cross-verified against Ballotpedia/official/news sources with no name discrepancies. Party filled where documented (metro/urban counties Democratic; verified rural counties Republican); left blank where not independently verified rather than assumed. Decatur County's Wiley Griffin is an Independent (party left blank, noted). Towns County turnover: elected Sheriff Kenneth Henderson (R, re-elected 2024) is suspended by Gov. Kemp (executive orders Oct 2025 and June 29, 2026) and Anthony Coleman serves as interim/acting sheriff as of 2026-07-03; the elected incumbent is recorded here. Other recent turnovers reflected: Richmond (Brantley, Jan 2025), Houston (Moulton, Jan 2025, succeeding late Sheriff Cullen Talton), Dougherty (Hayes, Dec 2024), Chatham (Coleman, Jan 2025), Thomas (Watkins, Dec 2024).
